checkpoint_context.active_guardrail_note: This family is admitted only with tighter proof-only guardrails and may not widen itself into deterministic replication, authority transfer, trust convergence, or live cutover realization.

verification_class_identifiers.description: Verify whether replay-relevant history remains reconstructable across a bounded distributed horizon.
Verify whether declared proof-anchor chains remain attributable, coherent, and non-contradictory.
Verify bounded continuity claims across more than one shard, partition, or authority-region-adjacent horizon without claiming live handoff.
Verify whether replay windows are lawfully equivalent, superseding, or continuity-linked under explicit criteria.
Verify explicit refusal, blocked, or out-of-scope outcomes when continuity criteria are missing or contradictory.
Verify that proof packets preserve explicit non-realization posture and do not drift into operational claims.

evidence_proof_class_identifiers.description: Evidence identifying replay horizon, sequencing posture, lifecycle posture, and replay boundary markers.
Evidence linking replay claims to lawful checkpoints, snapshots, or continuity anchors without collapsing their meanings.
Evidence showing declared lineage posture among proof anchors, replay windows, receipts, and authority-region identity.
Evidence that continuity claims are contradictory, incomplete, non-equivalent, or non-reconstructable.
Evidence that canonical operator or generalized live-cutover receipt lineage remains reconstructable where required.
Evidence that the lawful result is refusal, blocked status, partial status, or out-of-scope classification.
Evidence that a claim drifted into replication, transfer, convergence, or other later-wave realization territory.

blocker_baseline_dependence.gate_freeze_dependency: This family remains subordinate to the Z-P2 admitted_with_cautions gate and the tighter proof-only guardrails frozen by C-ZETA_A_ADMISSION_REVIEW.

runtime: Isolation, coexistence, hotswap boundaries, and distributed-authority foundations remain binding.
No verification packet may hide truth in undocumented local state.
No continuity proof may present checkpoint or cross-shard substrate as if authority handoff were already lawful.
release_control: Operator transaction doctrine remains upstream where relevant.
Generalized live-cutover receipts and provenance remain upstream.
Release contract profile, release-index semantics, and exact bounded scope remain reconstructable where required.
Archive presence, mirror visibility, ordinary logs, and CI output are insufficient as canonical distributed continuity proof.
trust: Trust-relevant conditions may be checked only within bounded proof-only posture.
Distributed trust convergence, live revocation propagation, live trust execution, and publication execution remain unrealized.
Trust-aware verification is not trust realization.

doctrine_admission_verification_realizat.doctrine: Defines what distributed replay and proof-anchor verification constitutionally means.
doctrine_admission_verification_realizat.admission: The family is admitted only with cautions inside the bounded first-wave perimeter.
doctrine_admission_verification_realizat.verification: The family may produce replay, anchor-chain, continuity, equivalence, refusal, and explicit non-realization verification outcomes.
doctrine_admission_verification_realizat.realization: Deterministic replication, state-partition transfer, distributed trust convergence, live cutover pipeline realization, and broader distributed live operations remain outside this family.

invalidity_failure_categories.description: The work drifted into replication, transfer, convergence, or live cutover claims.
Only local traces, partial replay windows, or non-canonical evidence were provided.
Declared proof-anchor linkage is absent, contradictory, or non-reconstructable.
Replay windows claimed as equivalent are not lawfully comparable.
Continuity claim conflicts with event, message, checkpoint, snapshot, or receipt posture.
Canonical linkage to operator or generalized receipt/provenance posture is absent where required.
Verification packet silently turned into trust realization or revocation realization.
The candidate belongs to later-wave, blocked, or future-only families instead of this one.
The proof packet is insufficient to support either affirmative continuity or a narrower lawful conclusion.

canonical: upstream checkpoint, blocker, matrix, gate, and Z-A0 doctrine
canonical verification packets, refusal packets, and bounded proof packets later defined under this doctrine
canonical operator transaction and generalized receipt records where relevant

downstream_refinement_prompts: later bounded Ζ-A family doctrine prompts
later broader Ζ planning that must preserve proof-only versus realization distinctions
